definition: A series of games where winners advance until one champion remains.
example: The chess tournament lasted all weekend.
etymology: From Old French torneiement — a mock battle or contest.
usageNote: Often used for sports, but also applies to academic or gaming events.

definition: A natural helper in cells that speeds chemical reactions.
example: Saliva contains enzymes that begin digestion.
usageNote: Enzymes work best at specific temperatures — that's why fever can affect digestion.

definition: Software used to visit websites on the internet.
example: She cleared cookies in her browser settings.
usageNote: Keep your browser updated to patch security holes.

definition: To soak food in a flavored liquid before cooking.
example: Let the tofu marinate for one hour.
etymology: From Latin marinare — to pickle in brine.
usageNote: Acidic marinades (lemon, vinegar) tenderize meat over time.

definition: Active mostly at night.
example: Owls are nocturnal hunters.
usageNote: Pair with diurnal (active by day) when comparing animal habits.

definition: An educated guess you can test with an experiment.
example: Our hypothesis was that plants grow faster with more light.
etymology: From Greek hypo (under) + thesis (placement) — a foundation for investigation.
usageNote: A hypothesis must be testable; if you can't measure it, revise the wording.

definition: The ability to keep going through tiredness or difficulty.
example: Marathon runners build endurance with long practice runs.
usageNote: Used for both physical sports and mental challenges like studying.

definition: A step-by-step set of rules a computer follows to solve a problem.
example: The sorting algorithm arranged names from A to Z.
usageNote: Algorithms appear in apps, games, and even recipes — any ordered steps count.

definition: To cook liquid just below boiling so small bubbles rise gently.
example: Simmer the soup for twenty minutes to blend the flavors.
usageNote: Simmering extracts flavor without the harsh boil that can toughen meat.

definition: Colors or patterns that help an animal blend into its surroundings.
example: The chameleon's camouflage hid it on the green leaf.
usageNote: Camouflage can be visual, but some insects use smell or sound to hide too.

definition: An official who enforces rules during a game.
example: The referee blew the whistle for a foul.
usageNote: Referees must stay neutral — their calls keep competition fair.

definition: To copy data from the internet onto your device.
example: Download the assignment before the Wi-Fi drops.
usageNote: Check file size before downloading on mobile data to avoid extra charges.

definition: The natural home where a plant or animal normally lives.
example: Wetlands are a vital habitat for migrating birds.
usageNote: Destroying habitats is a leading cause of endangered species.

definition: A substance in food that helps your body grow and stay healthy.
example: Leafy greens pack nutrients like iron and vitamin K.
usageNote: Balanced meals combine proteins, carbs, fats, and micronutrients.

definition: Speed in a specific direction — how fast and which way something moves.
example: The pitcher's velocity topped ninety miles per hour.
usageNote: Velocity includes direction; speed alone does not.
